test_frameworks.name: promptfoo
test_frameworks.scope: LLM eval harness for skills owned by this repository. Run via: cd tests/evals && npm run eval

applications.name: engineering-skills plugin
applications.description: Single plugin-source repository with Claude and Codex manifests, canonical skills under skills/, and a promptfoo eval harness under tests/evals/.

claude_manifest.description: Claude plugin manifest for this repository.

codex_manifest.description: Codex plugin manifest for this repository.

skills.description: Canonical skill directories (16 skills). Each skill owns its SKILL.md and any supporting references, assets, scripts, or agents.

eval_harness.description: Promptfoo harness and deterministic compatibility checks for skills in this repository. Agentic eval packages use a local OpenCode CLI provider that invokes `opencode run` directly, avoiding a managed `opencode serve` process. Current packages cover the Linear workflow skills, create-feature-request, code-simplifier, maintain-github-repos, writing-tests, adversarial-review, authoring-flow-spec, explaining-code, the 4 e2e BDD authoring skills, yolo, bundled raising-linear-pr promptfoo DB gate helper tests, eval coverage reporting, and Codex compatibility.

scripts.description: Repository helper and validation scripts. worktree.sh creates or reattaches sibling worktrees, symlinks shared local state such as .env and tests/evals/.promptfoo, allows direnv, and bootstraps tests/evals dependencies. validate_plugin_manifests.py checks Claude/Codex manifest required fields, identity, license, version, and skills-path parity. check_plugin_version_bump.py enforces shared Claude/Codex version equality and an origin/main version increase.

validator_tests.description: Unit tests for repository validation scripts.

commands.description: Top-level slash-command wrappers. Thin delegators to the skills under skills/. First entry: /author-flow-spec → authoring-flow-spec skill.

key_directories.skills/: All skill content owned by this plugin repo
key_directories.commands/: Top-level slash-command wrappers that delegate to skills
key_directories.tests/evals/: Promptfoo eval packages, prompts, assertions, a local OpenCode CLI provider, and wrapper script for skill regression checks
key_directories.scripts/: Repository helper and validation scripts such as canonical worktree setup and plugin manifest checks
key_directories.tests/: Unit tests for repository validation scripts

conventions.skill_location: All first-party skills live under skills/<skill-name>/
conventions.supporting_material: References, assets, agents, and scripts stay inside the owning skill directory when needed
conventions.worktrees: Use sibling worktrees at ../worktrees/<branch-name> via scripts/worktree.sh <branch-name>
conventions.local_dev_symlinks: For direct local use, symlink skills/<skill-name> into ~/.claude/skills/<skill-name> and ~/.codex/skills/<skill-name>
conventions.cross_repo_dependencies: Do not add references that require files outside this repository
conventions.codex_repo_guidance: Codex uses AGENTS.md directly as the repo-local guidance surface; there is no separate Codex adapter file in this repository

notes_for_agents.startup: Read this file before exploring the repository. It is the primary startup context for structure, ownership, and eval commands.
notes_for_agents.marketplace_model: This repository is a plugin source, not a marketplace. Marketplaces in other repositories should point to this repo as the plugin source.
notes_for_agents.eval_scope: tests/evals only covers skills owned by this repository. Do not move in evals for external skill repos such as superpowers.
notes_for_agents.update_repo_map: Refresh this file whenever manifests, skill layout, eval layout, validation scripts, or command references change.
notes_for_agents.license: Root repository content is Elastic License 2.0 (ELv2).
